report_name: Net Dollar Retention (NDR) / Net Revenue Retention (NRR)
executive_questions_answered: What percentage of starting revenue did we retain after upsells and churn?
Is our net retention improving or declining over time?
Are expansions making up for losses from churn?

metric_definitions.definition: Percentage of starting ARR retained after adding expansion (upsells/cross-sells) and subtracting churn and downgrades.
metric_definitions.formula: NRR = ((Starting ARR + Expansion ARR - Churned ARR) / Starting ARR) * 100

known_variants.definition_delta: Measures retention excluding expansions – i.e., revenue retained from existing customers without upsell
known_variants.when_to_use: Assessing base stability (pure churn rate) without growth effects

pitfalls: High NRR can mask churn if expansions from a few big customers offset many losses
Focusing only on NRR might overlook logo churn – use GRR to ensure broad retention, not just upsell-driven
Be clear on currency and timeframe (monthly vs annual NRR can differ if seasonality or upgrades mid-period)

required_fields.description: ARR at the beginning of the measurement period
ARR at the end of the measurement period

joins_and_keys.notes: Link subscriptions to account to aggregate ARR per customer

transformation_rules: Exclude new customers added during the period from the calculation (since NRR looks at starting cohort)
Use constant currency or FX-neutral values if international (so currency fluctuations don't skew retention)
If a customer expands and later churns in same period, count both expansion and churn appropriately in numerator

data_quality_checks.logic: Verify accounts with 0 ARR at start of period are excluded from NRR calculation
If NRR >> 100% (e.g., 150%+), confirm large expansions are real and not data errors

aggregation_rules: Compute NRR at account level then weighted average by Starting ARR, or equivalently sum expansion and churn across all accounts and plug into formula
Segment NRR by customer cohort or segment for deeper insight (optional)
calculations_and_models.cohort_logic: Optionally track NRR for cohorts (e.g., customers by initial signup year) to see retention by vintage

layout.purpose: Trend lines of net and gross retention rates over time
Show expansion added and churn lost in dollar terms for the period

narrative_insights_to_generate: Explain whether expansions outweighed churn or vice versa in contributing to NRR
Identify any segments with notably higher or lower NRR and possible reasons
diagnostic_questions: Which customer tier has the weakest GRR, and is upsell in that tier masking churn?
Are we relying on a few big expansions to prop up NRR, and what is underlying churn if those are excluded?

recommended_actions.action: Initiate churn reduction initiative for at-risk segment
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: GRR drops below threshold (e.g., <90%) even if overall NRR is high

recommended_actions.action: Expand customer success reach for growth opportunities
recommended_actions.trigger_condition: NRR growth is plateauing (expansion slowing relative to prior periods)

implementation_notes.minimum_viable_version: Simple calculation of NRR and GRR for total company on a quarterly basis
implementation_notes.advanced_version: NRR broken out by segment and product, with predictive churn flags and LTV impact analysis
common_client_customizations: Logo retention (customer count retention) shown alongside dollar retention
Exclude churn from specific low-end customer tiers in one view to understand core-market retention
privacy_and_access_notes: Detailed account-level retention data should be restricted to CS team; executives see aggregated rates
When presenting externally, ensure calculations match any public definitions used in investor reporting
success_criteria: Internal NRR calculations match those used in official financial reporting (no discrepancies)
Improvement in NRR/GRR after targeted actions confirms report insights drove effective strategies

references.title: Net Revenue Retention vs. Gross Revenue Retention: Explained
